title: tephroseris papposa var. subcapitata
intro: Tephroseris papposa var. subcapitata, also known as the Alpine Hawkweed or Tephroseris, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to its high concentration of flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Tephroseris papposa var. subcapitata include wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address a range of health concerns.
